name: Flying Dutchman Burger
servings: 1 burger, plus extra sauce and onions

ingredients.ingredient: Neutral oil, such as vegetable or canola oil

step_sections.name: Special Sauce
steps: Make the special sauce by combining all the ingredients in a bowl. Taste and adjust seasoning, then store in the fridge.

step_sections.name: Prep
steps: Cut the onion into whole onion slices. Be gentle so the individual onion rings do not separate.

Weigh out two 2 oz portions of ground beef and form them into patties about the same size as your bun. You can use a cookie cutter, smash them with a spatula between burger paper or parchment paper, or shape them by hand.

step_sections.name: Burgers
steps: Heat a large skillet or griddle over medium heat. Add some oil, then add 2 onion slices. Cook them for a few minutes, then gently flip. Repeat until they are nicely browned. Remove from the pan.

Add the beef patties to the pan or griddle and season with salt and pepper. Cook for about 3 minutes. Right before flipping, squirt them with mustard. Flip, then add a slice of American cheese to each patty. Squirt a little water into the pan, cover, and let the cheese melt for about 1 minute. Remove from heat.
Sandwich the burger patties between the two grilled onion slices. Wrap in parchment or burger paper if possible. Add a dollop of sauce between each bite.
To add buns, dry toast them in a pan, then add some sauce to each bun, followed by the onion-wrapped Flying Dutchman.
